title: The Conversion of Abu al-'As
description: Abu al-'As remained in Mecca, and Zaynab remained with the Messenger of God in Medina, separated by Islam. Just before the conquest of Mecca, however, Abu al-'As went on a trading expedition to Syria. He was regarded as a trustworthy man, and he had with him, besides his own merchandise, goods belonging to other men of Quraysh which they had entrusted to him to trade with.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
